mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for future equity cash flows, directly compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes vs 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings declines, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and moderate CPI inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without pricing pressures, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by >0.5% over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the industrial production index during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of recovery-phase INDPRO surges, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes outside recovery regimes (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate falling below 2.5% establishes a low-discount-rate environment that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 2.5%, or the threshold effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes vs 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in <45% of payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and declining housing starts signals consumer substitution toward durable goods at the expense of real estate, indicating demand fragility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to achieve statistical significance (p < 0.05) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production combined with elevated CPI inflation during monetary tightening cycles indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of tightening-phase observations with this supply-inflation mix, or the joint signal's predictive power (Rank IC) drops below 0.03 outside tightening regimes.
